sourceMovie.title: Death of a Unicorn
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Death of a Unicorn works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The House
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Death of a Unicorn clicked for you, The House (2022) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and fantasy, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Charlie and the Chocolate Factory
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Death of a Unicorn clicked for you, Charlie and the Chocolate Factory (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Death of a Unicorn?
faq.answer: Try The House, Charlie and the Chocolate Factory, Shaun of the Dead—each shares DNA with Death of a Unicorn while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Death of a Unicorn?
faq.answer: Death of a Unicorn (2025) is best described as Horror, Fantasy, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Death of a Unicorn worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
